metadata.title: Mobile-platform code review sub-rubric
metadata.description: Mobile-specific code-quality criteria layered atop the universal base code rubric. Covers memory management (retain cycles), lifecycle handling (backgrounding, state restoration), platform API usage patterns, and threading/concurrency (main-thread blocking).

severity_rules.pattern: Closure or block captures `self` strongly, creating a retain cycle (Swift/Objective-C); or Android Activity/Fragment captured in a long-lived listener / static reference.
severity_rules.description: Retain cycles leak entire view-controller graphs and inflate memory until the OS terminates the app. The most common iOS leak source and a frequent Android one.
severity_rules.remediation: Use `[weak self]` capture lists in Swift closures; use WeakReference wrappers or lifecycle-scoped observers on Android; verify with leak-detection instrumentation.

severity_rules.pattern: View controller / fragment subscribes to a global publisher (NotificationCenter, EventBus) without unsubscribing in deinit / onDestroyView.
severity_rules.description: Orphaned subscriptions hold references after the view is gone, leaking memory and firing handlers against deallocated state — a frequent crash source.
severity_rules.remediation: Use Combine sinks stored in a cancellables Set tied to the lifecycle; on Android use viewLifecycleOwner.lifecycleScope or DisposableHandle in onDestroyView.

severity_rules.pattern: View controller holds a reference to data loaded from a previous instance; missing cleanup in viewWillDisappear / onStop leaves stale state on subsequent appearances.
severity_rules.description: Mobile lifecycle reuse (especially with iOS view-controller containment and Android ViewPager fragments) demands explicit cleanup; stale state surfaces as bugs that reproduce only on second-visit.
severity_rules.remediation: Reset stateful properties in viewWillDisappear / onStop; prefer @State / SavedStateHandle for view state with built-in lifecycle coupling.

severity_rules.pattern: Fragment / view-controller assumes its view hierarchy exists during async-callback completion (e.g., calls findViewById after onDestroyView, accesses @IBOutlet after viewDidDisappear).
severity_rules.description: Async callbacks routinely arrive after the view is gone; ignoring lifecycle leads to NullPointerException / fatalError crashes that look intermittent in production.
severity_rules.remediation: Guard the callback with view-bound checks (Android: `_binding != null`; iOS: weak self + isViewLoaded); cancel pending work in lifecycle-end methods.

severity_rules.pattern: Code calls deprecated platform APIs (e.g., UIAlertView on iOS, AsyncTask on Android) that the platform vendor has marked for removal.
severity_rules.description: Deprecated APIs ship until they don't. Continuing to call them blocks the next minimum-OS bump and earns store-review warnings.
severity_rules.remediation: Migrate to the supported equivalent (UIAlertController, kotlinx.coroutines / WorkManager); review SDK-deprecation notes per release.

severity_rules.pattern: App accesses sensitive APIs (location, camera, contacts, photos) without first checking and requesting authorisation; or assumes a permission grant persists forever.
severity_rules.description: Both platforms revoke permissions when the user changes a setting or after long inactivity. Code that assumes always-granted crashes or silently fails.
severity_rules.remediation: Always check authorisation status on every entry; handle .denied / PERMISSION_DENIED with a graceful fallback and a deep-link to Settings.

severity_rules.pattern: UI updates dispatched from a background thread (network completion, file I/O) without hopping back to the main queue / main thread.
severity_rules.description: UIKit and Android UI toolkits are explicitly main-thread-only; cross-thread mutation crashes the app or corrupts the view hierarchy silently.
severity_rules.remediation: Wrap UI mutations in DispatchQueue.main.async / runOnUiThread / withContext(Dispatchers.Main); enable Main-Thread Checker in Xcode and StrictMode on Android.

severity_rules.pattern: Synchronous I/O (file read, network call, database query) runs on the main thread, blocking the run loop and triggering UI jank or ANR dialogs.
severity_rules.description: Main-thread blocking >100ms causes visible jank; >5s on Android triggers an Application-Not-Responding dialog and a likely uninstall.
severity_rules.remediation: Use async I/O (URLSession async/await, Room suspend functions); profile with Instruments Time Profiler / Android Profiler.
